clusters.title: Zelenskyy replaces Ukraine military chief after protests
clusters.neutral_headline: Zelenskyy replaces Ukraine military chief after protests
bullet_summary: In a late-night TV address, Zelenskyy said he was appointing Mykhailo Drapatyi, a popular and innovative general, as the new head of Ukraine’s armed forces
Drapatyi would oversee a reorganisation of the army’s command structure, Ukraine’s president said
Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced that he will replace​ the commander-in-chief of the armed forces as a government shakeup continues
President ​Volodymyr ​Zelensky said on Tuesday that Major ​General ‌Mykhailo ⁠Drapaty ‌will take over as Ukraine's ⁠commander-in-chief after firing Soviet-trained top commander Oleksandr Syrsky

items.title: Zelenskyy replaces Ukraine military chief after protests
items.standfirst: Replaced military chief Syrskii clashed with former Defence Minister Fedorov, whose removal last week led to protests.
items.content: Replaced military chief Syrskii clashed with former Defence Minister Fedorov, whose removal last week led to protests.

items.title: Zelenskyy fires Ukraine’s army chief and offers Fedorov ‘prominent position’
items.standfirst: After nationwide protests Oleksandr Syrskyi is to be replaced in reshuffle and popular defence minister returns to governmentVolodymyr Zelenskyy has fired Ukraine’s commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi and offered a “prominent position” to the defence minister he sacked last week in a move that sparked nationwide protests.In a late-night TV address, Zelenskyy said he was appointing Mykhailo Drapatyi, a popular and innovative general, as the new head of Ukraine’s armed forces. Drapatyi would oversee a reorganisation of the army’s command structure, Ukraine’s president said. Continue reading...
items.content: After nationwide protests Oleksandr Syrskyi is to be replaced in reshuffle and popular defence minister returns to governmentVolodymyr Zelenskyy has fired Ukraine’s commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi and offered a “prominent position” to the defence minister he sacked last week in a move that sparked nationwide protests.In a late-night TV address, Zelenskyy said he was appointing Mykhailo Drapatyi, a popular and innovative general, as the new head of Ukraine’s armed forces. Drapatyi would oversee a reorganisation of the army’s command structure, Ukraine’s president said. Continue reading...

items.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires his military chief after protests and names a new one
items.standfirst: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi as commander-in-chief of Ukraine's armed forces following days of protests demanding his removal. Zelenskyy appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new army chief.
items.content: '/>President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi as commander-in-chief of Ukraine's armed forces following days of protests demanding his removal. Zelenskyy appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new army chief.(Image credit: Efrem Lukatsky)

items.title: Zelenskyy replaces Ukraine's top general after days of protests
items.standfirst: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced that he will replace​ the commander-in-chief of the armed forces as a government shakeup continues.
items.content: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced that he will replace​ the commander-in-chief of the armed forces as a government shakeup continues.

items.title: Zelenskyy replaces top Ukraine general after mass protests
items.standfirst: President chooses 43-year-old Mykhailo Drapatyi to replace Oleksandr Syrsky in generational shift in Kyiv’s military leadership
items.content: President chooses 43-year-old Mykhailo Drapatyi to replace Oleksandr Syrsky in generational shift in Kyiv’s military leadership

items.title: Zelensky fires army chief Oleksandr Syrsky, ‌a 'victory for protestors'
items.standfirst: President ​Volodymyr ​Zelensky said on Tuesday that Major ​General ‌Mykhailo ⁠Drapaty ‌will take over as Ukraine's ⁠commander-in-chief after firing Soviet-trained top commander Oleksandr Syrsky. "This is a victory for the protestors who took to the streets a week ago, when Zelensky fired Fedorov," says FRANCE 24 correspondent Gulliver Cragg. "Their main demand was that Syrsky be fired." He added that "Drapaty was the one Fedorov wanted to see in that position to replace Syrsky".
items.content: President ​Volodymyr ​Zelensky said on Tuesday that Major ​General ‌Mykhailo ⁠Drapaty ‌will take over as Ukraine's ⁠commander-in-chief after firing Soviet-trained top commander Oleksandr Syrsky. "This is a victory for the protestors who took to the streets a week ago, when Zelensky fired Fedorov," says FRANCE 24 correspondent Gulliver Cragg. "Their main demand was that Syrsky be fired." He added that "Drapaty was the one Fedorov wanted to see in that position to replace Syrsky".

items.title: Ukraine's Zelensky fires military chief after protests and names a replacement
items.standfirst: President Volodymyr Zelensky on Tuesday fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrsky as commander-in-chief of Ukraine’s armed forces, following days of mass protests in Kyiv and other cities nationwide demanding his removal. Zelenskyy appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new army chief, according to a statement on social media.
items.content: President Volodymyr Zelensky on Tuesday fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrsky as commander-in-chief of Ukraine’s armed forces, following days of mass protests in Kyiv and other cities nationwide demanding his removal. Zelenskyy appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new army chief, according to a statement on social media.

items.title: Zelensky sacks Ukraine army chief Syrsky after protests over rival minister's dismissal
items.standfirst: President Volodymyr Zelensky on Tuesday dismissed Ukraine's military head Oleksandr Syrsky following days of public pressure and named Major ​General ‌Mykhailo ⁠Drapaty, an ally of ousted defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov, as the new commander-in-chief.
items.content: President Volodymyr Zelensky on Tuesday dismissed Ukraine's military head Oleksandr Syrsky following days of public pressure and named Major ​General ‌Mykhailo ⁠Drapaty, an ally of ousted defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov, as the new commander-in-chief.

items.title: Zelensky sacks Ukraine's top army commander after days of protests
items.standfirst: Oleksandr Syrskyi is dismissed after backlash over the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.
items.content: Oleksandr Syrskyi is dismissed after backlash over the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

items.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy fires his commander in chief after protests and major rift
items.standfirst: The firing caps a major crisis for Volodymyr Zelenskyy after Ukrainians took to the streets to call for Oleksandr Syrskyi’s resignation.
items.content: The firing caps a major crisis for Volodymyr Zelenskyy after Ukrainians took to the streets to call for Oleksandr Syrskyi’s resignation.

items.title: Zelensky faces deepening political crisis as protests dig in
items.standfirst: In Ukraine, protesters say they’re giving Volodymyr Zelensky until Friday to re-instate the popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, and fire General Oleksandr Syrsky, the military chief of staff. The President removed Fedorov from his post last week, citing a clash between the two men. The protests have brought Syrsky’s unpopularity to the forefront. Through credited with stopping the Russian invaders at the gates of Kyiv in 2022, he’s since gained a reputation for brutality, and for wasting lives unnecessarily. He's also, as our correspondent Gulliver Cragg reports, accused of tolerance for corruption.
items.content: In Ukraine, protesters say they’re giving Volodymyr Zelensky until Friday to re-instate the popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, and fire General Oleksandr Syrsky, the military chief of staff. The President removed Fedorov from his post last week, citing a clash between the two men. The protests have brought Syrsky’s unpopularity to the forefront. Through credited with stopping the Russian invaders at the gates of Kyiv in 2022, he’s since gained a reputation for brutality, and for wasting lives unnecessarily. He's also, as our correspondent Gulliver Cragg reports, accused of tolerance for corruption.

items.title: France approves blanket social media ban for under-15s
items.standfirst: France has approved a blanket ban on under-15 children using social media, the first European country to introduce such a restriction.
items.content: France has approved a blanket ban on under-15 children using social media, the first European country to introduce such a restriction.

items.title: France becomes first EU country to ban social media access for under-15s
items.standfirst: Parliament votes to stop children using apps such as TikTok in policy championed by Emmanuel MacronFrance’s parliament has approved a bill banning social media access for children under 15, making it the first EU country to bar children from apps such as TikTok.The president, Emmanuel Macron, has championed the ban as a key reform of his final term in office and pledged to enforce it by September. Continue reading...
items.content: Parliament votes to stop children using apps such as TikTok in policy championed by Emmanuel MacronFrance’s parliament has approved a bill banning social media access for children under 15, making it the first EU country to bar children from apps such as TikTok.The president, Emmanuel Macron, has championed the ban as a key reform of his final term in office and pledged to enforce it by September. Continue reading...

items.title: French parliament backs social media ban for under-15s in EU first
items.standfirst: FRANCE 24's Clovis Casali reports from France's National Assembly, where lawmakers have approved a ban on social media for children and teenagers under-15, the first EU country to do so. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Philippe Coen, lawyer and founder of the NGO Respect Zone.
items.content: FRANCE 24's Clovis Casali reports from France's National Assembly, where lawmakers have approved a ban on social media for children and teenagers under-15, the first EU country to do so. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Philippe Coen, lawyer and founder of the NGO Respect Zone.

items.title: France Passes Bill to Ban Social Media for Children Under 15
items.standfirst: France passed legislation to restrict the use of social media for children under 15 years old, becoming the first major European country to do so in a move that may strain relations with US technology companies and President Donald Trump.
items.content: France passed legislation to restrict the use of social media for children under 15 years old, becoming the first major European country to do so in a move that may strain relations with US technology companies and President Donald Trump.

items.title: Russian warship carried out firing exercise off Plymouth coast

items.content: The Neustrashimy's live-fire exercise took place in international waters and lasted for 30 minutes.

items.standfirst: New defence secretary Wes Streeting denounces drill by Neustrashimy as ‘performative and irresponsible’A Russian warship fired its main cannon in a short notice test 45 miles south of Plymouth on Monday, the day Andy Burnham was appointed as prime minister.The Neustrashimy suddenly announced it intended to fire its guns in the morning and requested that a British warship, which had been tracking it, retreat a couple of miles to a safe distance. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian goods
clusters.neutral_headline: Donald Trump to impose 50% tariff on most Canadian goods, White House says
bullet_summary: The tariffs will hit a wide range of products, the White House said, including wine, hockey sticks and cement
Reported by NPR News, Guardian World, NBC World

items.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian goods
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump on Monday imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, declaring that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol and dairy products.
items.content: President Donald Trump on Monday imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, declaring that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol and dairy products.(Image credit: Adrian Wyld)

items.title: Donald Trump to impose 50% tariff on most Canadian goods, White House says
items.standfirst: Turmoil likely as Trump officials say Canada unfairly discriminated against US autos, alcohol and dairy productsDonald Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods in response to the country retaliating against previous US tariffs, the White House announced on Monday, declaring Canada has unfairly discriminated against American cars, alcohol and dairy products.The tariffs will hit a wide range of products, the White House said, including wine, hockey sticks and cement. They also include goods previously protected from import taxes under the United States-Mexico-Canada (USMCA) agreement. Continue reading...
items.content: Turmoil likely as Trump officials say Canada unfairly discriminated against US autos, alcohol and dairy productsDonald Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods in response to the country retaliating against previous US tariffs, the White House announced on Monday, declaring Canada has unfairly discriminated against American cars, alcohol and dairy products.The tariffs will hit a wide range of products, the White House said, including wine, hockey sticks and cement. They also include goods previously protected from import taxes under the United States-Mexico-Canada (USMCA) agreement. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on dairy, alcohol, and cars from Canada
items.standfirst: The Trump administration imposed 50% tariffs on three categories of imports from Canada, dairy, alcohol, and cars, citing discriminatory actions. NBC News' Monica Alba reports on how Trump is also considering additional tariffs due to the Canadian wildfires.
items.content: The Trump administration imposed 50% tariffs on three categories of imports from Canada, dairy, alcohol, and cars, citing discriminatory actions. NBC News' Monica Alba reports on how Trump is also considering additional tariffs due to the Canadian wildfires.

clusters.title: India's Cockroach protests vow to continue despite police crackdown
clusters.neutral_headline: India's Cockroach protests vow to continue despite police crackdown

items.title: India's Cockroach protests vow to continue despite police crackdown
items.standfirst: The leaders of India's youth-led Cockroach movement are vowing to continue protesting a day after police cracked down on demonstrators. They have been demanding the country's education minister, Dharmendra Pradhan, resign amid repeated leaks for entrance tests to medical schools. While two of the movement's members met with a government minister on July 20th, India's prime minister, Narendra Modi, is failing to address the issue for now. Delano D'Souza reports.
items.content: The leaders of India's youth-led Cockroach movement are vowing to continue protesting a day after police cracked down on demonstrators. They have been demanding the country's education minister, Dharmendra Pradhan, resign amid repeated leaks for entrance tests to medical schools. While two of the movement's members met with a government minister on July 20th, India's prime minister, Narendra Modi, is failing to address the issue for now. Delano D'Souza reports.

items.title: India's youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown
items.standfirst: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.
items.content: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government. (Image credit: Shekhar Yadav)
